frameworks.name: real-time fMRI neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: closed-loop brain imaging that lets patients modulate targeted neural activity therapeutically

frameworks.name: closed-loop brain modulation
frameworks.one_line: broader paradigm of direct activity regulation for mental disorder treatment

frameworks.name: machine learning pattern decoding
frameworks.one_line: advances enable targeting precise multivariate activity patterns for therapy

core_question: Can real-time fMRI neurofeedback serve as a rigorous, targeted therapeutic intervention for mental health disorders?
key_assumptions: mental disorders involve dysregulation of specific brain processes
direct modulation of neural activity can produce clinical benefit
double-blind placebo-controlled designs are feasible for neurofeedback
manipulations: neurofeedback target specification via machine learning
key_findings: neurofeedback historically lacked mainstream acceptance due to methodological issues
contemporary guidelines permit double-blind placebo-controlled neurofeedback trials
ML and statistics now allow targeting precise activity patterns therapeutically
interpretations: rt-fMRI neurofeedback is a viable rigorous treatment avenue for mental disorders
neurofeedback uniquely supports double-blind placebo control among psychological interventions
